takeaways: Senior housing occupancy hits 89% with new supply at its lowest since 2006 — 86% of investors plan to increase sector exposure in 2026 as rents surge 28.8% above pre-pandemic levels.
CRE debt markets are opening up fast: Fannie/Freddie boosted multifamily caps to $88B each (up 20%), and MBA projects a 24% jump in total lending volume as the 10-Year Treasury holds near 4%.
AI proptech VC hit $4.5B in 2025 (within a record $16.7B proptech total) — agentic AI is now automating complex real estate workflows from procurement to lease pricing.

items.headline: Senior Housing Occupancy Hits 89% as New Supply Falls to 2006 Lows
items.summary: Q4 2025 senior housing occupancy reached 89% while new inventory dropped to its lowest level since 2006, per NIC MAP and JLL data. Annual rent growth exceeded 4%, with average monthly rents now at $5,479 — up 28.8% from pre-pandemic levels. For Lloyd Jones, this supply-demand imbalance signals continued pricing power and strong NOI growth across the portfolio.

items.headline: 86% of Investors Plan to Boost Senior Living Allocations in 2026
items.summary: A JLL survey found 86% of investors intend to increase their senior living exposure in 2026, as post-COVID trepidation fades and steep acquisition discounts disappear. The 80+ population is projected to grow 36.6% over the next decade with 10,000+ Americans turning 65 daily. Operators face margin pressure from labor, energy, and insurance costs, but demographic tailwinds and constrained supply continue to favor well-capitalized owners.

items.headline: Fannie and Freddie Raise Multifamily Caps to $88B Each, Boosting CRE Liquidity
items.summary: Fannie Mae and Freddie Mac each increased their 2026 multifamily loan caps to $88 billion — a 20% boost — while the Mortgage Bankers Association projects a 24% increase in total CRE lending volume. The 10-Year Treasury has stabilized around 4.00–4.25%, and at least one 25-basis-point Fed cut is expected early in the year. For senior housing operators, improved debt availability means better refinancing terms and more competitive acquisition financing.

items.headline: CRE Transaction Volume Set to Jump 15–20% as Capital Re-enters Market
items.summary: Colliers forecasts a 15–20% increase in CRE sales volume in 2026 as institutional and cross-border capital returns. Marcus & Millichap reports improved access to debt capital with rising loan-to-value ratios and supportive underwriting. Commercial mortgage rates currently range from 4.99% to 8.75% depending on property type, creating a window for well-positioned buyers to lock in favorable terms before further rate compression.

items.headline: AI Proptech VC Hits $4.5B as Three New Unicorns Emerge Since July 2025
items.summary: Proptech investment reached $16.7B in 2025 (up 68% YoY) with AI-native companies capturing $4.5B of that total. Three new AI proptech unicorns have emerged since July 2025, including Kiavi ($350M raise in Feb 2026) and Bedrock Robotics ($1.75B valuation). Fifth Wall CEO Brendan Wallace notes that big real estate CEOs have realized in the last 12 months that point solutions are no longer sufficient — integrated AI platforms are the new standard.

items.headline: Agentic AI Now Automating Complex Real Estate Workflows at Scale
items.summary: ICSC reports that agentic AI — autonomous systems that execute multi-step tasks — is reshaping retail and real estate operations in 2026. Retailers and CRE operators are deploying AI agents for procurement, dynamic pricing, inventory management, and event programming. For senior housing operators, agentic AI could streamline resident intake, maintenance scheduling, and revenue management across portfolios.

items.headline: 44% of CRE Investment Committees Still Distrust AI-Generated Analysis
items.summary: Despite massive proptech investment, trust remains the primary barrier to deeper AI adoption in high-value CRE workflows. A Commercial Observer report found 44% of real estate firms say their investment committees distrust AI-generated analysis. The gap between AI capability and organizational trust presents both a risk and an opportunity — firms that build robust AI governance frameworks early will gain a competitive edge.

items.headline: HBR: LLMs Give 'Trendslop' — Not Real Strategy, Researchers Find
items.summary: Harvard Business Review published March 2026 research showing that leading LLMs (ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, Grok) consistently recommend trendy solutions over context-specific strategy across 15,000+ trials. Models favor differentiation over cost leadership and collaboration over competition regardless of business context. The takeaway for leaders: use AI to expand strategic options, but never outsource judgment to it.

items.headline: 71% of Leaders Under Increased Stress as U.S. Layoffs Hit 10-Year High
items.summary: DDI’s 2026 leadership report reveals 71% of leaders face increased stress levels, with 40% considering leaving their jobs. U.S. layoffs have surged to a 10-year high, leaving organizations running lean. Succession planning has shifted from an annual HR exercise to a core strategic capability. For senior housing operators battling staffing shortages, investing in manager retention and development is now a competitive necessity.

items.headline: 89% of Employees Perform Better When Prioritizing Wellbeing, Survey Finds
items.summary: Wellhub’s 2026 State of Work-Life Wellness report (5,000+ employees across 10 countries) found 89% perform better when prioritizing wellbeing, yet 90% experienced burnout symptoms in the past year. Employees with structured wellness programs report 21 percentage points higher wellbeing scores. Wellness-focused 'third places' — gyms, yoga studios, wellness hubs — are replacing bars and coffee shops as the new centers of workplace connection.

items.headline: Neurowellness and Nervous System Regulation Emerge as Top 2026 Trend
items.summary: The Global Wellness Summit’s 2026 trends report highlights 'neurowellness' — regulating the nervous system through consumer neurotech and somatic practices — as a breakthrough category. With digital life keeping many in a chronic fight-or-flight state, personalized wellness driven by AI analytics and at-home biomarker testing is making prevention-first health mainstream. For senior living communities, integrating wellness programming aligns with resident expectations and premium positioning.
